rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC.'S, A DELAWARE DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the 5th cause of action for inducing breach of contract to plead facts to support application of the discovery rule. Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the 10th and 11th causes of action to allege facts showing conduct that occurred in California. Norwest Mortgage, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 214, 222-223. The demurrer is overruled to the 8th and 9th causes of action because the UTSA does not preempt them and the 9th cause of action pleads sufficient facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOY ANDERSON VS. CITIAPARTMENTS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Establishing Admission, Comelling Responses To To Form Interrogatories, And Fo An Award Of Sanctions Against Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS JOY ANDERSON, NICOLAS HARR'S Motion Establishing Admission, Compelling Responses To To Form Interrogatories, And For An Award Of Sanctions Against Defendants. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The Requests for Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions in the amount of $2475 are ordered to be paid jointly and severally by Defendants and Defendants' counsels. Sanctions are due within 30 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH P. DIGGINS et al VS. SAVON CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT ARTHUR ABEZGAUZ' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT Granted. Defendant has met his burden of establishing that he lacked actual notice of the lawsuit, and it was not caused by avoidance of service of inexcusable neglect. (CCP § 437.5) Plaintiffs' objections to Defendant Abezgauz's Declaration in Support of the Motion to Set aside are overruled. Defendant's motion is timely and is accompanied by Defendant's affidavit, and proposed answer. (CCP § 375.5(a).) Defendant's affidavit states that he had no knowledge of the lawsuit before receiving the abstract of judgment, which was delivered to the address 55 Castlemont Avenue, Daly City, California. As a condition to the grant of this motion, defendants must pay to plaintiffs, delivered to the office of plaintiff's counsel by noon on Friday, October 4, 2013, reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $1,000. Any answer filed without compliance with this order shall be stricken on the court's motion or on ex parte application. A verified declaration showing payment shall be presented and filed at the rime of filing the answer to the complaint. See CCP 473.5(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: FISCAL FUNDING CO. INC. VS. ALLEN MATKIS LEEK GAMBRE MALLORY LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash And /Or Strike Or In The Alternative, Application For Writ Of Prohibition To Arrest Respondents' Motion To Amend Judgment To Add New Attorney Grigsby As New Petitioner And Request For Fees; And Cross Motion Motion To Amend Judgment To Strike Award Of Costs And Fees Not Based On Statutory Authority
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 5, PETITIONER FISCAL FUNDING CO. INC.'S Motion To Quash And/Or Strike Or In The Alternative, Application For Writ Of Prohibition To Arrest Respondents' Motion To Amend Judgment To Add New Attorney Grigsby As New Petitioner And Request For Fees; And Cross Motion To Amend Judgment To Strike Award Of Costs And Fees Not Based On Statutory Authority Denied. As to the Motion to Quash, Petitioner has made no showing that the Court lacks personal jurisdiction over him. As to the Motion to Strike, Respondent is not seeking to amend a pleading and therefore the statutes cited are inapplicable here. As to the Writ of Prohibition, CCP §1103(a) states that a Writ of Prohibition may only be issued to an inferior tribunal. As to the Cross-Motion to Amend the Judgment, Petitioner has cited no authority that would give the court the discretion to make such an amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: YASSER KHAIRI VS. RICHARD THOMAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Written Disco Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 2. DEFENDANTS EDWARD WONG, MARGARET WONG'S Motion To Compel Written Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond to the supplemental written discovery, including the supplemental interrogatory, supplemental document request and the form interrogatories. Further, the Requests for Admission are deemed admitted. Sanctions of $360.00 are awarded to the Defendant. The Responses and sanctions are ordered to be provided within 30 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CARIDEO VS. STANLEY KELLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Fo Rorder Compelling Responses To Demand For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 3. DEFENDANTS STANLEY KELLEY, ANTHONY KELLEY'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied without prejudice. The Court finds that neither the motion nor the responses are untimely; however, Defendant did not comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345(c) and the parties did not meet and confer in good faith with regard to the specific requests. No sanctions are awarded. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding these specific requests and the specific objections. Should there remain any disputes after a good faith meet and confer, the Court orders that a motion to compel may be brought with regard to those remaining requests no later than October 28, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANTOINE YORK VS. DESPERADOS MOTORCYCLE CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses By Pltf To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Req.'S For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 5. DEFENDANT CONTEMPORARY SERVICES CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Responses By Plaintiff To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied as moot. Plaintiff provided responses prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 8. DEFENDANT MATTHEW KESSLER'S Motion To Quash Subpoena And For A Protective Order And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. CHOW et al VS. TED WIN WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Ted Win Wong And Roger Louie'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 24, 2013, Line 22, Defendants Ted Win Wong And Roger Louie's Demurrer To Complaint Defendant's demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have not established that they are a real party in interest, as required by CCP §367. Nowhere in the Complaint does it establish that Plaintiffs are "members" of the Corporation. (See Cal. Corp. Code §5056(d), providing that a person who has voting rights by virtue of being a director is not a member under §5056(a).) Where a complaint states a cause of action in someone, but not in the plaintiff, a general demurrer for failure to state a cause of action will be sustained. (Klopstock v. Superior Court (1941) 17 Cal.2d 13, 19.) In the alternative, it is uncertain how Plaintiffs (and not the corporation) were injured by the actions of the two defendant board members. Where it cannot be ascertained from the allegations of the complaint how or in what manner the plaintiff has been damaged, a special demurrer for uncertainty is properly sustained. (Oppenheimer v. General Cable Corp. (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 293, 298-99.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
